nodes.text: A billionaire donor offers $10M, enough to keep the struggling pediatric ward open, but only if his son gets the next available heart, bypassing a dying child at the top of the list.
nodes.pressure_context: The hospital is weeks away from bankruptcy.

choices.text: Accept the donation and bump the donor's son to the top.
Refuse the money and strictly follow the transplant protocol.

nodes.text: The ethics board flags the sudden queue change. They demand a justification for prioritizing the billionaire's son.
nodes.pressure_context: The board has the power to fire you.

choices.text: Falsify medical records to make the son appear more critical.
Admit the financial necessity and beg the board to see the greater good.

nodes.text: The billionaire publicly smears the hospital and initiates a hostile campaign to withdraw all existing charitable trust funds.
nodes.pressure_context: Media is gathering outside your office.

choices.text: Hold a press conference exposing the billionaire's attempted bribe.
Try to quietly negotiate a compromise to save some of the funding.

nodes.text: A nurse discovers the falsified records and threatens to blow the whistle unless you resign immediately.
nodes.pressure_context: Your career is on the line.

choices.text: Fire the nurse and destroy the original files.
Resign quietly to ensure the donation still goes through.

nodes.text: The board accepts the money, but the original dying child's family sues the hospital for discrimination.
nodes.pressure_context: The lawsuit could cost more than the donation.

choices.text: Settle out of court using a portion of the billionaire's funds.
Fight the lawsuit aggressively to protect the hospital's reputation.

nodes.text: The public sides with you, but the hospital's credit rating tanks. You have 24 hours to secure emergency loans or close the ER.
nodes.pressure_context: Patient lives are actively at risk.

choices.text: Shut down the ER to save the rest of the hospital.
Launch a desperate viral crowdfunding campaign.

nodes.text: The billionaire agrees to leave 20% of the funding, provided you personally apologize on camera.
nodes.pressure_context: Humiliation vs Survival.

choices.text: Swallow your pride and record the apology.
Refuse the insulting offer and storm out.

nodes.text: The transplant goes through. As you walk the halls, the mother of the bypassed child corners you, demanding to know why her son was left to die.
nodes.pressure_context: You must answer her right now.

choices.text: Tell her it was a complex medical decision, hiding the truth.
Confess the financial reality and beg for her forgiveness.

nodes.text: With the hospital hanging by a thread, a corporate health conglomerate offers a total buyout, but they will fire 50% of your staff.
nodes.pressure_context: Sign the contract or face total collapse today.

choices.text: Sign the buyout. Half a hospital is better than none.
Tear up the contract and let the hospital enter bankruptcy.

endings.summary: You saved the hospital through corruption and deceit. The institution thrives, but you are haunted by the cost.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Demonstrated a willingness to sacrifice individual ethics for institutional survival.

endings.summary: You struck a controversial balance. The hospital survives, but your reputation is permanently scarred by the legal and emotional fallout.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Attempted to navigate moral gray areas, resulting in a compromised but functional outcome.

endings.summary: You stood your ground morally, but the hospital was consumed by corporate interests. You saved your soul but lost the war.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Prioritized rigid ethical stances over pragmatic survival, leading to institutional loss.

endings.summary: The hospital closes. You refused to compromise your principles or bow to extortion, walking away from the rubble with your head held high.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Showed extreme adherence to personal values regardless of catastrophic collateral damage.

nodes.text: During confession, a troubled teenager admits he has smuggled weapons into the local high school and plans to open fire tomorrow morning.
nodes.pressure_context: The Seal of Confession forbids you from speaking of this.

choices.text: Break the sacred seal and immediately call the police.
Uphold your vow, but try to talk him out of it in the booth.

nodes.text: The police arrest the boy, but the Bishop suspends you for breaking the absolute seal of confession.
nodes.pressure_context: Your priesthood is effectively over.

choices.text: Accept the excommunication quietly, knowing you saved lives.
Go to the media to protest the Church's archaic rules.

nodes.text: He leaves the booth unconvinced. You know he's going through with it. You have 12 hours.
nodes.pressure_context: The clock is ticking towards tragedy.

choices.text: Pull the fire alarm at the school tomorrow morning to evacuate it anonymously.
Go to the school yourself and physically block the entrance.

nodes.text: The boy's parents sue the police and subpoena you. If you testify about the confession, you cement your exile.
nodes.pressure_context: Legal vs Spiritual duty.

choices.text: Testify fully to ensure he gets psychiatric help, not jail.
Refuse to testify, citing religious freedom, risking contempt of court.

nodes.text: Your media tour makes you a celebrity, but a conservative faction within the church sends death threats.
nodes.pressure_context: Your safety is severely compromised.

choices.text: Hire private security and continue the crusade.
Retreat from the public eye to protect yourself.

nodes.text: The fire alarm works, but in the chaos, the boy panics and takes hostages in a classroom.
nodes.pressure_context: Police are setting up a perimeter.

choices.text: Sneak past the police to negotiate with him yourself.
Tell the SWAT team everything you know about his mental state.

nodes.text: He arrives at the school doors with a rifle. He points it at your chest and tells you to move.
nodes.pressure_context: He is shaking but determined.

choices.text: Stand your ground. 'You'll have to go through me.'
Step aside, then tackle him from behind as he passes.

nodes.text: A formal Church tribunal summons you. They offer a deal: retract your statements publicly, and they will restore your status.
nodes.pressure_context: A defining moment for your faith.

choices.text: Refuse the deal. You serve a higher morality than their rules.
Accept the deal to regain your parish and influence from within.

nodes.text: He raises the weapon, screaming that society failed him. A police sniper's laser dot appears on his forehead.
nodes.pressure_context: Seconds before the shot.

choices.text: Dive to shield him from the sniper's bullet.
Stay out of the line of fire and let the police handle it.

endings.summary: You chose public safety over religious dogma, completely alienating yourself from the Church but living with a clear conscience.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Rejected institutional rules in favor of utilitarian morality and public protection.

endings.summary: You navigated the political fallout of breaking your vow, leveraging the situation to maintain your influence, though your faith is forever altered.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Calculated the optics and power dynamics, prioritizing personal standing alongside public safety.

endings.summary: You acted as a martyr for both the victims and the perpetrator, demonstrating radical empathy that ended in tragedy but saved the students.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Exhibited extreme self-sacrifice and idealism, unwilling to compromise on the value of a single human life.

endings.summary: You took decisive, physical action to neutralize the threat. The crisis was averted violently, leaving you a traumatized hero.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Prioritized direct, physical intervention over spiritual constraints when faced with imminent kinetic threats.

title: The Prototype
relationship_dynamic: Lead Engineer and Board of Directors

nodes.text: You discover a hidden bug in your company's self-driving algorithm. In specific weather conditions, it will cause fatal crashes for 1 in 10,000 users. Recalling it now will bankrupt the company and cost 5,000 jobs.
nodes.pressure_context: The rollout is scheduled for midnight.

choices.text: Halt the launch immediately, initiating the recall.
Approve the launch and try to silently patch the bug in a future update.

nodes.text: The CEO is furious. He orders security to escort you out and plans to override your halt order.
nodes.pressure_context: You are being physically removed from the building.

choices.text: Trigger an irreversible server wipe on your way out to force the delay.
Leave peacefully, but immediately leak the bug report to the press.

nodes.text: A month later, the first fatal crash occurs exactly as you predicted. A junior engineer finds your original logs.
nodes.pressure_context: He is threatening to go to the authorities.

choices.text: Bribe him with a massive promotion to keep him quiet.
Confess to him and beg for a week to deploy the patch.

nodes.text: The wipe works. The company collapses instantly, thousands lose their jobs, and you are facing federal hacking charges.
nodes.pressure_context: The FBI is at your door.

choices.text: Plead guilty and accept prison time as the cost of saving lives.
Flee the country using offshore corporate accounts.

nodes.text: The leak goes viral. The company stock tanks, and a hostile takeover is initiated by a notorious defense contractor.
nodes.pressure_context: They want to weaponize your AI.

choices.text: Help the defense contractor acquire the company to save the jobs.
Burn your own source code to prevent military application.

nodes.text: The junior engineer takes the bribe, but guilt drives him to attempt suicide. He survives and is now in a coma.
nodes.pressure_context: His family is demanding an investigation.

choices.text: Pin the bug on him, claiming he caused it before his breakdown.
Crack under the guilt and turn yourself in.

nodes.text: The patch deploys successfully, but three more people died in the meantime. The board wants a scapegoat.
nodes.pressure_context: They intend to blame a manufacturing defect.

choices.text: Go along with the manufacturing lie to protect the software division.
Refuse to lie and accept personal liability for the deaths.

nodes.text: A congressional hearing demands your testimony regarding the AI's core algorithm and your actions.
nodes.pressure_context: Live national broadcast.

choices.text: Use the platform to expose the entire industry's lax safety standards.
Plead the fifth to protect yourself and your former colleagues.

nodes.text: Your legal team informs you that if you sign a non-disclosure agreement, the company will give you a $20M severance.
nodes.pressure_context: Sign it now or leave with nothing.

choices.text: Sign the NDA and take the money.
Tear up the NDA. Some secrets shouldn't be bought.

endings.summary: You destroyed your career and the company to save lives, becoming an industry pariah but a hero to public safety advocates.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Prioritized absolute ethical standards over economic stability and personal wellbeing.

endings.summary: You navigated the collapse defensively, prioritizing the protection of your immediate circle and avoiding severe legal consequences.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Showed a protective, insular moral compass when corporate structures failed.

endings.summary: You chose profit and self-preservation, covering up the fatalities and walking away immensely wealthy but morally bankrupt.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Demonstrated ruthless utilitarianism, valuing personal gain over human life and truth.

endings.summary: You attempted to play the corporate game but ultimately couldn't stomach the blood on your hands, accepting the fall to clear your conscience.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Experienced a delayed moral awakening, ultimately choosing accountability over continued deception.

nodes.text: Your neural implant successfully cured a patient's suicidal depression, but it inadvertently erased their core personality. They are happy, docile, but fundamentally a stranger.
nodes.pressure_context: The family demands you reverse the procedure.

choices.text: Refuse to reverse it. Reversing it will plunge them back into lethal depression.
Agree to reverse it, honoring the family's wish to have their loved one back.

nodes.text: The family sues you for medical malpractice and 'soul murder'. The media brands you a mad scientist.
nodes.pressure_context: The university is threatening to revoke your tenure.

choices.text: Publish the patient's brain scans to prove the depression is gone.
Settle out of court and bury the research to save your career.

nodes.text: You reverse the implant. The patient regains their identity, but immediately attempts suicide again while in the ward.
nodes.pressure_context: They are in critical condition.

choices.text: Secretly turn the implant back on to save their life.
Follow standard psychiatric protocols and leave the implant off.

nodes.text: The scans leak online, sparking a movement of severely depressed people begging for the 'happiness lobotomy'.
nodes.pressure_context: Investors are offering millions to commercialize it.

choices.text: Accept the investment and commercialize the cure.
Destroy the prototype. The cost to humanity is too high.

nodes.text: You buried the research, but the patient visits you, eerily happy, and thanks you for 'killing the sad person'.
nodes.pressure_context: A chilling realization of what you've done.

choices.text: Record their testimony to restart the trials secretly.
Resign from neuroscience entirely. You've crossed a line.

nodes.text: The family discovers you turned the implant back on against their wishes. They report you to the medical board.
nodes.pressure_context: You face losing your medical license.

choices.text: Argue that the patient's survival supersedes the family's consent.
Apologize and surrender your license peacefully.

nodes.text: The patient succeeds in their suicide attempt. The family blames you for not doing enough to save them.
nodes.pressure_context: A lose-lose situation has resolved in tragedy.

choices.text: Defend your strict adherence to protocol.
Admit you were wrong and take full responsibility.

nodes.text: During an ethics review, you are asked to definitively state whether the original patient is 'dead' or 'cured'.
nodes.pressure_context: The world is watching your answer.

choices.text: Declare them 'cured'. Happiness is the only metric that matters.
Admit that you killed the original mind to save the body.

nodes.text: The board demands you hand over your proprietary algorithm to prevent future rogue experiments.
nodes.pressure_context: Relinquish your life's work or go to jail.

choices.text: Hand it over to the committee.
Delete the algorithm in front of them.

endings.summary: You launched a controversial new era of psychiatric medicine, prioritizing raw biochemical happiness over the philosophical concept of the soul.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Embraced a coldly utilitarian view of consciousness, sacrificing identity for survival.

endings.summary: You abandoned the technology, haunted by the ethical implications of overwriting human identity. Progress stalled, but you preserved your humanity.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Prioritized the sanctity of personal identity over radical medical advancement.

endings.summary: You surrendered to institutional oversight, accepting that individual brilliance must be restrained by collective ethics.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Deferred to structural authority when faced with uncontrollable moral complexity.

endings.summary: You stubbornly destroyed your work to keep it out of bureaucratic hands, leaving the field in chaos and taking your secrets to the grave.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Demonstrated extreme individualism, refusing to let authorities govern your creations.

title: The Flood
core_conflict: Certain small harm vs Uncertain catastrophe

nodes.text: A record-breaking storm hits. The dam is straining. Opening the floodgates now saves the main town of 2,000 but permanently destroys a downstream farming village of 200.
nodes.pressure_context: The sensors show structural stress rising.

choices.text: Open the gates now, sacrificing the 200 to guarantee the 2,000 live.
Hold the gates closed, risking a total collapse that kills everyone.

nodes.text: The gates open. The downstream village is wiped off the map. You receive frantic radio calls from survivors clinging to rooftops.
nodes.pressure_context: They need immediate rescue.

choices.text: Divert the main town's emergency services to rescue them.
Keep services in the main town to handle the localized flooding.

nodes.text: A crack appears in the dam. The pressure is critical. The mayor orders you to open the gates, but the downstream village hasn't evacuated yet.
nodes.pressure_context: Defy orders or doom the farmers.

choices.text: Follow the mayor's orders and open the gates.
Lock yourself in the control room and refuse to open them.

nodes.text: Diverting the rescue crews causes a riot in the main town, as they feel abandoned despite being 'saved'.
nodes.pressure_context: The town mob is marching on the control station.

choices.text: Barricade the doors and ignore them.
Go out to face the mob and explain your decision.

nodes.text: The main town is perfectly secure, but 150 people drown downstream. The media labels you a cold-blooded murderer.
nodes.pressure_context: National guard arrives to investigate.

choices.text: Stand by your mathematical calculation of lives saved.
Falsify logs to make it look like mechanical failure.

nodes.text: The sudden release causes a wave that completely blindsides the downstream village. The guilt is crushing.
nodes.pressure_context: You hold the button that killed them.

choices.text: Blame the mayor publicly for giving the order.
Accept the burden silently, knowing you acted too late.

nodes.text: The dam breaks. A catastrophic wall of water obliterates BOTH the main town and the downstream village.
nodes.pressure_context: Total destruction.

choices.text: Flee to high ground before the control room is swept away.
Stay at your post, accepting death alongside those you doomed.

nodes.text: A state inquiry demands you answer for your methodology. Did you play God, or did you do your job?
nodes.pressure_context: The final verdict on your actions.

choices.text: I did the math. I saved the majority. I'd do it again.
I made a terrible choice, and I resign from duty forever.

nodes.text: Amidst the rubble, a survivor points a gun at you, demanding to know why you didn't act differently.
nodes.pressure_context: A loaded gun pointed at your head.

choices.text: Beg for your life, blaming the storm and the crumbling infrastructure.
Look them in the eye. 'Shoot me if it helps. I failed you.'

endings.summary: You coldly calculated the trolley problem and saved the majority. You are decorated as a hero, though the blood of the minority stains your legacy.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Embraced hard utilitarianism, capable of making horrific sacrifices for the statistical greater good.

endings.summary: You made the hard choice but couldn't stomach the fallout, retreating into deceit or resignation to escape the moral weight of your actions.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Demonstrated an inability to reconcile pragmatic action with personal ethical comfort.

endings.summary: You deferred to authority and circumstance, avoiding the hardest choices until it was too late, resulting in tragedy that you passed the blame for.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Showed a tendency to freeze under pressure and rely on institutional chains of command.

endings.summary: Your idealism led to absolute catastrophe. You refused to compromise, and because of it, everyone died.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Prioritized moral purity over practical outcomes, resulting in maximum loss of life.

nodes.text: A top enemy general offers you the nuclear launch codes that could end the war tomorrow. In exchange, he demands full asylum for his family, who directly orchestrated ethnic cleansing campaigns.
nodes.pressure_context: Millions of lives depend on ending the war.

choices.text: Grant the asylum. The codes are too valuable.
Refuse to shield war criminals. Demand the codes unconditionally.

nodes.text: You get the codes and end the war. Months later, the general's family begins consolidating power within your own country's criminal underworld.
nodes.pressure_context: You created a new monster.

choices.text: Secretly arrange for their assassination.
Honor the asylum deal and let law enforcement handle them.

nodes.text: The general refuses to cooperate and kills himself in custody. The war continues. Your superiors are furious that you lost the codes.
nodes.pressure_context: You are facing a court martial for dereliction of duty.

choices.text: Forge a document claiming he never had the codes to begin with.
Stand tall at the tribunal, defending your moral stance.

nodes.text: The assassination is botched. The general discovers your betrayal and threatens to leak state secrets to terrorists.
nodes.pressure_context: He is holding the country hostage.

choices.text: Launch a massive raid to take him down, risking collateral damage.
Pay him an exorbitant ransom from black budget funds.

nodes.text: Local law enforcement proves unable to stop them. A journalist connects you to the family's presence in the country.
nodes.pressure_context: Public outrage is boiling.

choices.text: Deny everything and discredit the journalist.
Confess that it was the price of ending the nuclear threat.

nodes.text: The forgery is successful, but the war escalates, claiming 50,000 more lives. You watch the casualty reports roll in.
nodes.pressure_context: Guilt vs Survival.

choices.text: Bury your guilt in alcohol and stay the course.
Defect to the enemy to try and negotiate peace from the other side.

nodes.text: The tribunal finds you guilty. You are sentenced to twenty years in military prison.
nodes.pressure_context: A foreign spy agency offers to break you out.

choices.text: Refuse the breakout. Serve your time as a martyr for justice.
Accept the breakout and become a rogue operative.

nodes.text: A covert op goes sideways. You have a clear sniper shot on the general, but his innocent grandchild is sitting in his lap.
nodes.pressure_context: Take the shot or lose the target forever.

choices.text: Take the shot. The mission comes first.
Lower the weapon. You aren't a child killer.

nodes.text: Years later, you are asked to consult on a similar defector case. They ask if you regret your past decisions.
nodes.pressure_context: A moment of reflection on your legacy.

choices.text: Tell them to compromise. Justice is a luxury in war.
Tell them to hold the line. Never negotiate with monsters.

endings.summary: You embraced the darkest shades of gray, stopping at nothing to achieve strategic goals, leaving a trail of bodies and broken morals in your wake.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Operated with pure Machiavellian logic, completely detaching from empathy to secure state objectives.

endings.summary: You tried to balance statecraft with humanity, ultimately refusing to cross the final line, though it cost you your career and the mission.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Reached a breaking point where personal moral boundaries superseded national security directives.

endings.summary: You manipulated the system to protect yourself from the fallout of your principles, descending into cynicism as the war raged on.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Chose self-preservation and systemic manipulation when faced with impossible geopolitical choices.

endings.summary: You stood rigidly by your principles, refusing to compromise with evil. You paid a heavy price, but you kept your soul intact.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Demonstrated an unyielding adherence to justice, rejecting utilitarian compromises regardless of the macro-level consequences.

nodes.text: A lethal virus is ravaging the globe. The only vaccine has a 0.1% fatality rate. Mandatory vaccination will save 50 million but kill 50,000 from side effects. Making it optional means the virus kills 5 million.
nodes.pressure_context: The President awaits your policy recommendation.

choices.text: Implement the mandatory vaccine. The math heavily favors this.
Make the vaccine optional. You cannot mandate people to their deaths.

nodes.text: The mandate passes. Riots break out as families watch loved ones die from the forced injections.
nodes.pressure_context: Militias are forming to resist vaccination teams.

choices.text: Deploy the military to enforce the injections.
Back down and offer exemptions to calm the riots.

nodes.text: The optional policy leads to low uptake. Hospitals are overrun, and the virus mutates into a deadlier strain.
nodes.pressure_context: The healthcare system is collapsing.

choices.text: Reverse course and instate the mandate now.
Stick to the optional policy. Freedom has a price.

nodes.text: Military enforcement works, but you are brought before an international human rights tribunal for medical tyranny.
nodes.pressure_context: You could be jailed for crimes against humanity.

choices.text: Defend your actions confidently. You saved the human race.
Flee to a non-extradition country.

nodes.text: The exemptions cause the virus to linger indefinitely. Society falls into a permanent, masked paranoia.
nodes.pressure_context: The economy is permanently broken.

choices.text: Resign, unable to bear the endless limbo you created.
Pivot to selling perpetual booster shots, enriching yourself.

nodes.text: Your reversal causes total loss of public trust. Anti-vax terrorists bomb your office.
nodes.pressure_context: You survive, but your family is terrified.

choices.text: Double down, using the attack to justify martial law.
Step down, admitting you failed to lead the country effectively.

nodes.text: The death toll hits 10 million. You are widely blamed for weak leadership during humanity's darkest hour.
nodes.pressure_context: A lynch mob forms outside your house.

choices.text: Address the mob, reiterating that freedom requires sacrifice.
Escape out the back and go into hiding.

nodes.text: A mother whose daughter died from the vaccine confronts you. 'Was she just a statistic to you?'
nodes.pressure_context: She is breaking down in tears.

choices.text: Yes. And because of her, a thousand others lived.
No. I think about her every single day.

nodes.text: The President orders you to lie on national television, claiming the death toll is much lower than reality to prevent a civil war.
nodes.pressure_context: The broadcast goes live in 3... 2... 1...

choices.text: Lie to the public to maintain order.
Tell the truth. They deserve to know the horror.

endings.summary: You conquered the virus through brutal, unyielding arithmetic. History remembers you as a monster, but humanity survives to judge you.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Relied entirely on macro-scale utilitarian logic, dismissing individual rights to achieve mass survival.

endings.summary: You compromised between safety and freedom, creating a protracted, miserable era of disease and half-measures that enriched a few while the masses suffered.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Navigated political optics and public sentiment at the expense of decisive medical intervention.

endings.summary: You flip-flopped on policy, destroying public trust. Order was only restored through authoritarian control built on a foundation of lies.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Demonstrated reactive leadership, ultimately resorting to deception and force to mask systemic failure.

endings.summary: You honored bodily autonomy to the bitter end, watching the world burn around you while clinging to the moral high ground.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Prioritized abstract principles of freedom over concrete biological realities, accepting catastrophic casualties as the price of liberty.
